steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the dimensions
steps.content: The width is $w=8$ cm. The length is $45$ cm longer than the width, so $\ell = 8 + 45 = 53$ cm.

steps.title: Apply the area formula
steps.content: Area of a rectangle is $A = \ell \cdot w$, so $A = 53 \cdot 8 = 424$ square cm. That matches Choice B — $424$.
